title: Frostmourne
timelinePosition: Opens the morning after Chapter 20's landfall at Daggercap Bay and closes on the retreat from the Frostmourne cavern toward Muradin's camp.
transport: Harbor movement on foot -> forced march inland through Dragonblight -> approach to Muradin's camp -> cavern assault and fighting retreat.
immediateObjective: Track Arthas inland fast enough to reach him before whatever is calling him in the glacier finishes the turn.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
No interior access to Jaina, Muradin, or Arthas; Jaina's discoveries must read through speech, gesture, breath, and spell behavior only.
Do not reintroduce Daggercap Bay, Northrend, or Jaina's widened Frostmourne sensitivity as first discoveries; chapter21 inherits them from chapter20.
Keep the cave loss tactical rather than melodramatic; the chapter proves the partnership by how cleanly it loses and withdraws.

povKnowsAtOpen: Arthas passed through Daggercap Bay weeks ago and pushed inland with speed and purpose.
Jaina's Frostmourne sensitivity is sharper and more dangerous after the crossing, but it is also their best inland guide.
No institution is between them and Northrend now; the pursuit belongs entirely to the formation.
Arthas's expedition left military traces strong enough to follow.
The source wanted Arthas to arrive and did not want Sylvanas and Jaina to arrive, which means something specific waits inland for him.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: What object or revelation Arthas is actually chasing.
Whether any local ally survived long enough to brief them.
Whether the prince is still a man they can reach before the turn completes.
What exact seam Jaina will later hear inside the blade's field.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No assumption that Muradin dies; the chapter ends with him wounded and handed off.
No suggestion that the cave loss means the partnership failed.
No clean labeling of the Lich King's whole hierarchy beyond what field evidence supports.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Arthas has already claimed Frostmourne and is no longer operating like a prince.
Muradin can explain the blade, the route, and the curse, but he cannot stop the prince before the cave.
Jaina's Scholomance education is what lets her identify Frostmourne as the plague's sister-weapon.
The cave fight proves the partnership at its best and also proves that skill alone is not enough against the blade.
Muradin falls but is recovered alive enough for handoff.
Jaina heard a seam in Frostmourne's field, and that thread now matters more than any straight rematch.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: They are late, but lateness does not yet mean irrelevance if the prince can still be reached before the source finishes with him.
pov.surfaceObjective: Turn harbor traces into a usable inland line and reach Arthas before the unknown north of the bay hardens around him.
pov.pressureSource: Arthas's lead, Jaina's depletion, Northrend terrain, and the private knowledge that Sylvanas helped set this prince on the path she is now racing to cut.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Some part of Sylvanas still wants superior execution to recover time that raw arithmetic has already lost.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can recognize a clean defeat for what it is without turning that defeat into blame against the partner beside her.
pov.forcedChoice: Enter the cave despite the too-late evidence, then choose retreat quickly enough to save the formation when the matchup proves unwinnable.
pov.endStateShift: Sylvanas stops treating Arthas as the primary target and starts treating the blade as the actual enemy.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 22 opens with Muradin wounded, Jaina carrying a new thread in her head, and the race home fully alive.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Tracking instrument, magical striker, and the only person in the formation who can hear what Frostmourne actually is.
throughPov.povPressure: Sylvanas reads the mage through bread-smell, air-writing, tremor, and the exact expressions that mean Jaina has found something she cannot yet explain.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Jaina's discoveries are visible only through what she says, how fast she says it, and what her hands start doing when her mind outruns speech.

team.label: partnership / team
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Fast, disciplined, and already carrying the weight of the voyage.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The chapter gives the partnership its outside witness in Muradin, then proves its ceiling inside the cave.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the cave loss itself prove the quality of the pair. Do not narrate admiration ahead of the evidence.

entries.rule: Blunt, practical, warm in the dwarf way, and capable of naming what Sylvanas and Jaina will not name about themselves.
Flank security, undead suppression, casualty extraction. Their professionalism keeps the retreat clean.
Brief harbor presence only; she matters as exit strategy, not as inland participant.
Read only through Sylvanas's face-reading, Jaina's magical warnings, and the blade's effects. No interior access.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 22 opens with Muradin's handoff, the return march, and Jaina starting to turn what she heard in the cave into something usable.

scenes.title: The Burned Shore
scenes.sceneFunction: Let the harbor itself tell the story of Arthas's inland push before any ally speaks.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas came through this harbor weeks ago, and the source lies inland.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep burned hulls, hasty dockwork, abandoned camp traces, and inland tracks on page so Arthas's lead feels physical.

enrichment.detail: Daggercap Bay should read as a frozen withdrawal site: charred hull ribs, hurried dockwork, ration traces, stripped camp remains, and a harbor reused for campaign movement rather than safety.
enrichment.source: Planner enrichment from chapter21 notes, grounded in stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md

beats.goal: Read Daggercap Bay for evidence of Arthas's movement and state of mind.
beats.conflict: The harbor confirms not just that Arthas came through, but that he was already treating his own people as expendable fuel.

outcome.summary: The bay yields a clean picture of the expedition's route inland, but the burned ships make clear that the prince they are following is already operating beyond ordinary command logic.

beats.valueShift: late arrival -> the lead becomes uglier
newOnPageInformation: Arthas burned most of his own fleet after landing.
The bay still shows supply traces and hurried military use rather than simple transit.
The Wolfheart survives only because it arrived after the burning, not because the harbor is safe.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What exactly Arthas raced inland to reach.
Whether anyone local survived with useful intelligence.
sensoryAnchors: Charred ship ribs frozen into harbor ice.
Burn smell still lingering in cold air weeks later.
Intact Wolfheart hull set against wrecks that carried no one home.
beats.transitionIntent: action-continuation: once the harbor evidence clarifies Arthas's burned withdrawal, the thread shifts inland to the only line he left behind.
beats.verifierNotes: Treat the charred-hull read as inherited chapter20 harbor evidence clarified by daylight, not as a first reveal of the bay's damage.
beats.verifierModification: Retagged the opener as callback continuity so chapter21 inherits chapter20's landfall evidence instead of rediscovering it.

beats.goal: Pick up the inland line and decide whether the formation can still make contact before the target hardens beyond reach.
beats.conflict: Everything about the track says brutality, speed, and no intent to circle back; the trail itself argues they are already late.

outcome.summary: The line inland is obvious and followable, but the evidence says Arthas drove his people at the pace of obsession rather than campaign logic.

beats.valueShift: the lead becomes uglier -> the chase becomes a race
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's track is too broad and too hard-run to read as cautious advance.
Jaina's widened channel aligns with the same inland line the bootprints mark.
No sign suggests hesitation, fallback, or supply-minded pacing.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How far inland the decisive site lies.
Whether the formation reaches it before the prince claims what he came for.
sensoryAnchors: Frozen bootprints and dragged sled lines in dirty snow.
Torn packing straw and broken crate hoops at the edge of camp.
Jaina's expression changing when the magical line and the physical line match.
beats.transitionIntent: environmental-pressure: once the trail and the widened channel agree, Northrend itself has to start taxing the formation before any ally arrives.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's widened channel is already baseline after the crossing; this beat should read as escalation and directional alignment, not first onset.

sceneLevelWarnings: Harbor evidence and Jaina's channel need to feel inherited from chapter20 rather than staged as fresh surprise.

scenes.title: The March
scenes.sceneFunction: Make the land hostile, then deliver the last surviving local intelligence.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The line inland is clear and the target is moving fast.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep terrain, cold, exhaustion, and line-of-march discipline visible so the cave does not feel like a teleport.

enrichment.detail: Dragonblight should play first as an attrition corridor: dead-white distance, punishing footing, thin shelter, and the sense that the land wants living bodies ground down before anything dramatic happens.
enrichment.source: Planner enrichment from chapter21 notes, grounded in stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md

beats.goal: Cross the glacial interior fast enough to keep the race alive.
beats.conflict: Dragonblight slows everyone except the source and the man following it; terrain, cold, and Jaina's post-crossing condition all tax the formation.

outcome.summary: The formation keeps Farstrider pace on bad ground, but every mile deepens the sense that the land itself is aligned against the living.

newOnPageInformation: Northrend's interior reads as a dead zone shaped for attrition rather than habitation.
Jaina hears the source more clearly the farther they push inland.
Sylvanas can feel the place pressing through ward-sense even without arcane training.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the target ahead is still a prince or already something else.
Whether the cave can be reached before the turn finishes.
sensoryAnchors: Hard snow crust breaking under boots.
Grey-white distance with no soft edges.
Breath freezing faster than speech can finish.
beats.transitionIntent: relief-then-reversal: after the land strips the formation down to pace and endurance, the next movement offers a living foothold that may still already be too late.
beats.verifierNotes: Northrend is already established; deepen into interior attrition and worsening proximity instead of reintroducing the continent as abstract dread.

beats.goal: Reach the dwarven camp and test whether it is still staffed by the living.
beats.conflict: The camp is built to survive siege conditions and will not trust strangers quickly, especially in a landscape that keeps turning living things into less-than-living things.

outcome.summary: The formation reaches the camp and gains entry, but the camp's state makes clear that everyone here has already been living under the same warning Sylvanas and Jaina only just arrived to read.

newOnPageInformation: The camp still holds because dwarven discipline and fortification bought time.
The survivors already understand they are dealing with cursed territory rather than a normal campaign.
Arthas passed through here recently enough that the intelligence is still hot.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much the surviving dwarves actually know about the object Arthas sought.
Whether any warning they provide can still beat Arthas to the cave.
sensoryAnchors: Timber palisade rimed white.
Smoke from low dwarven cookfires under windbreaks.
Men on the wall looking relieved and suspicious in equal measure.
beats.transitionIntent: handoff: reaching the camp transfers the chapter from hostile travel to local intelligence, with Muradin as the person who can name the blade and the pair from outside.

scenes.title: The Ally
scenes.sceneFunction: Deliver the Frostmourne intelligence, let Muradin crack the pair open from the outside, and commit the trio to the race.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas passed this camp and headed deeper inland after something worth fortifying against.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep Muradin's physical presence, camp heat, and the arithmetic of time visible so the alliance never turns chatty.

enrichment.detail: Muradin's camp should feel like a siege-worn dwarven dig turned field fort: practical heat, rough timber, stored iron, tired discipline, and no wasted coziness.
enrichment.source: Planner enrichment from chapter21 notes, grounded in stories/the-plague-road/characters/muradin.md and location references

beats.goal: Secure Muradin's cooperation and absorb the last clear read from someone who knew Arthas before the cave.
beats.conflict: Muradin's news is useful, but it comes with the outside naming Sylvanas and Jaina have been avoiding and with the time loss the camp cannot erase.

outcome.summary: Muradin trusts them quickly and gives them the local picture, but he also makes plain that Arthas is already near whatever called him north.

beats.valueShift: local foothold -> outside witness to the partnership
newOnPageInformation: Muradin has watched Arthas deteriorate up close and no longer believes ordinary command logic applies.
He is the first outsider to say, plainly, that Jaina and Sylvanas move like a machine built from two people.
The camp cannot save the time already lost; it can only spend its last useful intelligence on them.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the named object can still be reached before Arthas touches it.
Whether Muradin joins as guide, witness, or casualty-in-waiting.
sensoryAnchors: Hot metal-tasting ale in a shelter that still smells of forge and cold wool.
Muradin's beard-rings clicking while he talks.
Sylvanas hearing the outsider word what the pair never would.
beats.transitionIntent: dialogue-hook: once Muradin names the pair's machine-like coordination and Arthas's decline, the room can finally turn to the weapon explaining the route.
beats.verifierNotes: Muradin is newly on-page, but the coordination he names is established from chapter20; keep his observation as outside witness rather than first proof.

beats.goal: Learn what Frostmourne is and why it matters.
beats.conflict: The briefing completes the picture at exactly the moment when the picture may already be too late to change.

outcome.summary: The intelligence is complete enough to matter, and it lands with the knowledge that Arthas left for the blade two days ago.

beats.valueShift: outside witness -> the blade becomes the real target
newOnPageInformation: Frostmourne is a cursed weapon calling to Arthas from the glacier.
Jaina recognizes the blade's resonance family as kin to the plague's mechanism.
Muradin has been warning anyone who would listen for months and found no language that mattered until Jaina gives him one.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas has already claimed the blade by the time they arrive.
What the blade does to a wielder in real time rather than in warning text.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's fingers writing invisible notation in cold air.
Muradin's relief at being told not just that the blade is wrong but why.
Frost on the shelter wall while the name finally lands.
beats.transitionIntent: action-continuation: once Frostmourne is named as the real target, briefing has to become movement before the last margin closes.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's resonance-family recognition must extend the already-established plague-mechanism language rather than introduce a new explanatory framework from nowhere.
beats.verifierModification: Added a continuity guard so Frostmourne's naming lands as a new object threaded through established resonance theory, not as replacement lore.

beats.goal: Turn the briefing into movement before the last narrow chance disappears.
beats.conflict: The arithmetic is terrible - Arthas left two days ago for a three-day march that a lighter force might cover faster.

outcome.summary: The trio forms and the race begins, but everyone in the room knows the margin is already thin enough to tear.

beats.valueShift: the blade becomes the real target -> last-chance pursuit
newOnPageInformation: Muradin joins personally rather than staying behind the walls.
The formation becomes briefly larger and more capable without becoming safe.
The road to the cave is now measured in hours and half-days, not in abstract chapters.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether they are racing a prince or only racing the aftermath of a prince.
Whether the trio survives first contact with the blade lane.
sensoryAnchors: Muradin's hammer settling on its harness as he stands.
Snow kicked up by a departure that does not pause for a meal or a speech.
Jaina agreeing with too little breath to waste words.
beats.transitionIntent: deadline-carry: committing Muradin personally turns theory into timed pursuit, so the next beat must cash that arithmetic at the cave.

scenes.title: Frostmourne
scenes.sceneFunction: Deliver the too-late proof, prove the partnership's ceiling, and let the first real anti-blade thread survive the loss.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Frostmourne exists, Arthas ran for it, and the trio is almost certainly late.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep Light's Vengeance, the soldiers in the cave, the anti-magic field, and the extraction discipline concrete so the loss feels tactical rather than decorative.

enrichment.detail: Keep Frostmourne Cavern grounded in load-bearing specifics: Light's Vengeance outside, warning-inscription logic, soldiers already going wrong before full death, and the cave itself as a weaponized environment.
enrichment.source: Planner enrichment from chapter21 notes, grounded in stories/the-plague-road/plans/frostmourne-lure.md

beats.goal: Reach the cave and judge whether entry still has any chance of changing the outcome.
beats.conflict: Everything outside the entrance already says they are too late - tracks in, no tracks out, and Arthas's holy hammer discarded in the snow.

outcome.summary: The formation reaches the cave, and the evidence outside it confirms the prince they chased is already gone in the only way that matters.

beats.valueShift: last-chance pursuit -> threshold to a finished turn
newOnPageInformation: Light's Vengeance lies abandoned in the snow outside the cave.
Even Sylvanas can feel the wrongness radiating out of the entrance.
Jaina hears the blade without trying, which means the channel is now receiving rather than only searching.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anything of Arthas can still be reached inside.
Whether the cave offers any tactical advantage at all.
sensoryAnchors: Golden hammer glow against blue-white snow.
Bootprints going in and never coming back out.
The ward-sense telling Sylvanas to leave before she crosses the threshold.
beats.transitionIntent: threshold-pressure: the too-late evidence outside the cave forces the formation to enter anyway, carrying dread and obligation across the threshold.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina receiving the blade without probing should read as escalation of the widened channel chapter20 already locked in place.

beats.goal: Confirm what Arthas has become and what Frostmourne does to the men nearest it.
beats.conflict: The main chamber shows the investigation's target already transformed, surrounded by soldiers who look living at a glance and wrong at a second look.

outcome.summary: The chamber gives them total clarity and zero comfort; Arthas is holding the blade, and his first words confirm there is no misunderstanding left.

beats.valueShift: threshold -> confrontation with the transformed target
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's loyal soldiers stand changed by proximity even before death fully takes them.
Frostmourne's cold is an active power, not just an atmosphere effect.
Arthas's face is still his and the decision-lag inside it is gone.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any attack lane can touch him cleanly.
Whether the formation can do more than prove the loss.
sensoryAnchors: Sourceless blue-white light under glacial ice.
Men in armor standing too still to be ordinary soldiers.
Arthas's voice sounding like his with the warmth removed.
beats.transitionIntent: action-escalation: once the room reveals Arthas and the blade in full, the only remaining test is whether peak coordination can touch him at all.

beats.goal: Stop Arthas in the cave before he can leave with the blade.
beats.conflict: Frostmourne's field eats Jaina's best magic, Arthas's enhanced physicality overruns mortal training, and the cave itself becomes a weapon when he turns the blade against it.

outcome.summary: The partnership fights at absolute peak and still loses the matchup, and Muradin takes the shard hit that breaks the trio apart.

newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas's best opening arrow proves ordinary lethal standards no longer apply.
Jaina can map the field fast enough to know its family resemblance to the plague, but not fast enough to break it here.
Muradin falls to ice-shard blowback when Arthas uses the cave itself as his answer.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Muradin is dead or only unconscious.
Whether the formation can carry anything useful out of a fight it lost.
sensoryAnchors: First arrow clattering on ice after Arthas pulls it out.
Magic breaking against the blade's field before it reaches flesh.
Shattered ice flying like thrown knives when Frostmourne strikes stone.
beats.transitionIntent: consequence-carry: the failed matchup turns immediately into extraction logic, forcing the chapter to prove the retreat is as disciplined as the assault.

beats.emotion: Defeat processed at operational speed - no room for grief, only for extraction, counting, and the first shape of a different answer.
beats.dilemma: Stay and lose everyone in a rematch they cannot win, or retreat cleanly with the wounded and accept that the next attempt has to use a different weapon than skill.
beats.decision: Retreat now, save Muradin, and carry the one thin thread Jaina heard in the blade's cycle back out into the wind.
beats.valueShift: clean defeat -> resolve built on a new thread
newOnPageInformation: The formation extracts with all seven bodies, one unconscious and none abandoned.
Sylvanas's post-fight reading is brutally clear: she would not change the positioning, the timing, or the partner.
Jaina starts air-writing immediately on the march out, which means the cave gave her something even while it beat them.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Muradin survives the carry south.
What, exactly, the seam Jaina heard can become once she has room to think.
sensoryAnchors: Dwarf boots dragging tracks in snow while elves carry him.
Light's Vengeance left glowing behind them because there is no hand free for it.
Jaina's shaking fingers drawing invisible patterns even while she walks.
beats.transitionIntent: handoff: chapter22 opens on the southbound carry with Muradin alive enough to hand off and Jaina already working the seam the cave exposed.
beats.verifierNotes: Sylvanas refusing to make Jaina the problem is an evolution of chapter20's trust-under-command, not a sudden emotional thaw.
beats.verifierModification: Strengthened the sequel handoff so chapter22 inherits active seam-work, Muradin's living extraction state, and the partnership's no-blame verdict without recap.

sceneLevelWarnings: Keep the defeat tactical and fast; the chapter's only full sequel is beat11, so the cave cannot sprawl into repetitive exchange.

activeCastAtClose: Arthas (behind them in the cavern with Frostmourne)
factsLockedForChapter22: Arthas claimed Frostmourne before the formation arrived.
The cave fight proved the partnership's peak and proved it was not enough on its own.
Muradin is badly wounded but recovered from the cave.
Jaina heard an oscillation seam in Frostmourne's field and is already processing it.
Light's Vengeance remains outside the cave, unclaimed.
The next chapter begins with extraction, warning attempts, and the race home.
factsNotYetEarned: Any claim that Muradin dies from the cave wound.
Any finished spell or usable throne-room choreography.
Any institutional listener who immediately believes the warning.
Any narrowing of Jaina's widened sensitivity.

warnings: The chapter runs ten scene beats before its first sequel; that breakneck shape is usable, but it still risks flattening unless harbor evidence, march attrition, briefing, and cave defeat feel sharply differentiated.
Harbor evidence and Jaina's sensitivity must read as carry-forward or worsening, not as first revelation.

Potential canon divergence remains around Muradin's wounding sequence at Frostmourne; keep it consistent with the established outline and handoff rather than trying to rewrite canon inside the verifier pass.
The Muradin scene is the chapter's only real breathing pocket before the cave; if the camp becomes chatty rather than time-pressured, the whole back half loses urgency.
The retreat must preserve tactical clarity and all-seven extraction so chapter22 inherits clean aftermath rather than ambiguous cave chaos.
normalizations: Beat 5 was normalized from a hybrid scene/sequel label to a scene beat because it already carries goal, conflict, and outcome structure.
Because chapter21-initial.json was missing, this verified plan reconstructs the normalized JSON scaffold directly from the approved outline plus verifier annotations.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter20's night landfall: after the Wolfheart reaches Daggercap Bay and the harbor already shows charred fleet remains, the chapter reopens the next morning with solid ground underfoot, Jaina's widened channel still humming at baseline, and Sylvanas reading the burned shore for the inland line Arthas left behind.

craftResearch.topic: fantasy opening chapters and opening hooks
craftResearch.findings: Open in active contact with a problem, pose the chapter's central question immediately, and avoid explanatory warm-up before the first pressure lands.
Travel beats stay alive when environment, obstacles, and route choices keep changing the pressure instead of summarizing distance.
Escalating action needs changing tactical problems, clear spatial stakes, and one decisive mismatch point rather than repetitive exchange.

craftResearch.topic: defeat endings that propel the next chapter
craftResearch.findings: A losing climax still needs consequence, choice, and a surviving thread that makes the reader want the next move immediately.
Competence scenes land hardest when outside witnesses, operational choices, and aftermath force characters to reveal what they trust under pressure.

pacing.notes: The chapter sustains a clear rise from harbor read to attrition march to briefing pivot to cave defeat. The breakneck ten-scene-beat run is deliberate rather than flat because each pressure source changes shape: evidence, environment, time arithmetic, threshold dread, then clean tactical loss.

structure.notes: Beat 1 opens in active evidence-reading instead of recap, beats 5-6 form the midchapter turn that reclassifies Arthas's chase into Frostmourne pursuit, and beat 11 closes on a forward-driving retreat that hands directly into chapter22.

character.notes: Sylvanas drives every scene through decisions, movement, and tactical reading; Jaina stays external-only and legible through behavior; Muradin has a distinct narrative job as local briefer and outside witness rather than redundant companion. The chapter test lands at beat 11, where Sylvanas treats the defeat as real without converting it into blame against Jaina.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified and retagged into explicit causal carries. No gaps remained after strengthening the cave-retreat handoff, and the chapterBridge now cleanly connects chapter20's landfall to chapter21's morning-after harbor opener.

continuity.notes: 5 new beats, 1 callback, 5 evolutions. No contradictions were found in the targeted character, location, relationship, magic, or timeline memory files. Anti-reintroduction guards were added for Daggercap Bay, Northrend, Jaina's widened channel, and the already-proven partnership dynamic.

modifications.description: Added a direct chapterBridge from chapter20's landfall to chapter21's morning-after harbor read so the opener inherits the Wolfheart arrival, the charred fleet evidence, and Jaina's already-widened channel instead of resetting the state.

Retyped every beat's transitionIntent into explicit causal carries and strengthened beat11's handoff so chapter22 can open on extraction, Muradin's survival window, and active seam-work without recap.

Corrected the opening and retreat annotations so inherited harbor evidence, Jaina's widened sensitivity, and the partnership's trust-under-command read as callback or evolution rather than fresh revelation.
